data.content:
This is an **Android tombstone** crash from a .NET MAUI app using the **Mono runtime** (`libmonosgen-2.0.so`). Let me extract the key details and attempt to download symbols for symbolication.

**Crash Summary:**
- **App**: `com.companyname.MauiTest`
- **Signal**: SIGABRT — abort message: `"not compatible"`
- **Runtime**: Mono (`libmonosgen-2.0.so`)
- **Build ID**: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`
- **Frames #01–#07** are .NET runtime (Mono) frames; **#08** is JIT-compiled managed code.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i llvm | head -5

# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using ELF Build ID
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"
OUTDIR="/tmp/sv-964e3fbd12c447618889ee4162779600/symbols"
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# Try different URL formats on the Microsoft symbol server
for fmt in "elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}" "elf-buildid-${BUILDID}" "${BUILDID}ffffffff"; do
  URL="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/${fmt}/${FILENAME}"
  echo "Trying: $URL"
  HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.${fmt}" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL" 2>&1)
  echo "  HTTP $HTTP_CODE ($(stat -c%s "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.${fmt}" 2>/dev/null || echo 0) bytes)"
  if [ "$HTTP_CODE" != "200" ]; then
    rm -f "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.${fmt}"
  fi
done

# Also try .dbg suffix
for fmt in "elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}" "elf-buildid-${BUILDID}"; do
  URL="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}.dbg/${fmt}/${FILENAME}.dbg"
  echo "Trying: $URL"
  HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.dbg.${fmt}" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL" 2>&1)
  echo "  HTTP $HTTP_CODE ($(stat -c%s "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.dbg.${fmt}" 2>/dev/null || echo 0) bytes)"
  if [ "$HTTP_CODE" != "200" ]; then
    rm -f "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.dbg.${fmt}"
  fi
done

# Try _.debug suffix (debuginfod convention)
URL="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo "Trying: $URL"
HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o "$OUTDIR/_.debug" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL" 2>&1)
echo "  HTTP $HTTP_CODE"
[ "$HTTP_CODE" != "200" ] && rm -f "$OUTDIR/_.debug"

ls -la "$OUTDIR/" 2>/dev/null

data.reasoningText:
This is an Android tombstone/crash log, not an Apple .ips crash log. The apple-crash-symbolication skill is specifically for Apple platforms (iOS, tvOS, Mac Catalyst, macOS), so it won't help here.

Let me analyze this crash log:

1. **Platform**: Android (arm64), running on an emulator
2. **App**: `com.companyname.MauiTest` - a .NET MAUI app
3. **Signal**: SIGABRT with abort message "not compatible"
4. **Runtime**: Mono (`libmonosgen-2.0.so`) - this is the Mono runtime used on Android for .NET MAUI apps


5. **Build ID**: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`

The crash is happening in the .NET runtime frames (#01-#07 in `libmonosgen-2.0.so`), with frame #08 likely being JIT-compiled managed code in anonymous memory. To symbolicate these Android native frames, I'd need the debug symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` and can use tools like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to resolve the addresses.

The Build ID can help locate matching symbols from Microsoft's symbol server, which publishes ELF debug symbols using a specific URL format based on the filename and Build ID. attempt to download the symbol file and then use addr2line or llvm-symbolizer to symbolicate the addresses. I should also check whether either of those tools is available on the system first.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i llvm | head -5

# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using ELF Build ID
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"
OUTDIR="/tmp/sv-964e3fbd12c447618889ee4162779600/symbols"
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# Try different URL formats on the Microsoft symbol server
for fmt in "elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}" "elf-buildid-${BUILDID}" "${BUILDID}ffffffff"; do
  URL="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/${fmt}/${FILENAME}"
  echo "Trying: $URL"
  HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.${fmt}" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL" 2>&1)
  echo "  HTTP $HTTP_CODE ($(stat -c%s "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.${fmt}" 2>/dev/null || echo 0) bytes)"
  if [ "$HTTP_CODE" != "200" ]; then
    rm -f "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.${fmt}"
  fi
done

# Also try .dbg suffix
for fmt in "elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}" "elf-buildid-${BUILDID}"; do
  URL="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}.dbg/${fmt}/${FILENAME}.dbg"
  echo "Trying: $URL"
  HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.dbg.${fmt}" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL" 2>&1)
  echo "  HTTP $HTTP_CODE ($(stat -c%s "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.dbg.${fmt}" 2>/dev/null || echo 0) bytes)"
  if [ "$HTTP_CODE" != "200" ]; then
    rm -f "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.dbg.${fmt}"
  fi
done

# Try _.debug suffix (debuginfod convention)
URL="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo "Trying: $URL"
HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o "$OUTDIR/_.debug" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL" 2>&1)
echo "  HTTP $HTTP_CODE"
[ "$HTTP_CODE" != "200" ] && rm -f "$OUTDIR/_.debug"

ls -la "$OUTDIR/" 2>/dev/null

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo \"---\"; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i llvm | head -5", "description": "Check for symbolication tools", "initial_wait": 10}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args:
{"command": "# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using ELF Build ID
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"
OUTDIR="/tmp/sv-964e3fbd12c447618889ee4162779600/symbols"
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# Try different URL formats on the Microsoft symbol server
for fmt in "elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}" "elf-buildid-${BUILDID}" "${BUILDID}ffffffff"; do
  URL="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/${fmt}/${FILENAME}"
  echo "Trying: $URL"
  HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.${fmt}" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL" 2>&1)
  echo "  HTTP $HTTP_CODE ($(stat -c%s "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.${fmt}" 2>/dev/null || echo 0) bytes)"
  if [ "$HTTP_CODE" != "200" ]; then
    rm -f "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.${fmt}"
  fi
done

# Also try .dbg suffix
for fmt in "elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}" "elf-buildid-${BUILDID}"; do
  URL="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}.dbg/${fmt}/${FILENAME}.dbg"
  echo "Trying: $URL"
  HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.dbg.${fmt}" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL" 2>&1)
  echo "  HTTP $HTTP_CODE ($(stat -c%s "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.dbg.${fmt}" 2>/dev/null || echo 0) bytes)"
  if [ "$HTTP_CODE" != "200" ]; then
    rm -f "$OUTDIR/${FILENAME}.dbg.${fmt}"
  fi
done

# Try _.debug suffix (debuginfod convention)
URL="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo "Trying: $URL"
HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o "$OUTDIR/_.debug" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL" 2>&1)
echo "  HTTP $HTTP_CODE"
[ "$HTTP_CODE" != "200" ] && rm -f "$OUTDIR/_.debug"

ls -la "$OUTDIR/" 2>/dev/null", "description": "Download Mono symbols from Microsoft", "initial_wait": 30}

toolArgs.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i llvm | head -5
